shape_semantics: Joint-necessity. code over (cmp,vp,vpc,naive) = L,L,W,L: ONLY value_profile_cmplog resolves; cmplog, value_profile, and naive all block. Every branch carries TWO Pass-A signatures of ONE joint mechanism: an h0 (vpc>value_profile, attributed to I2S => I2S necessary) and an h1 (vpc>cmplog, attributed to CMP_MAP gradient => value_profile necessary). The shape itself (Layer 1) already asserts both techniques are jointly required; the evidence test does NOT re-confirm jointness (that would violate G2 by verifying vpc-resolves with vpc-resolves). Instead it discriminates WHICH joint subtype each branch is, on a measurement INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern: it reads the structural/value content of the cmplog-BLOCKING and value_profile-BLOCKING seed sets, the whole-corpus retention footprint of the gradient, OR (round 3) the per-seed iteration DEPTH each arm's corpus drives the branch to, and asks what the gradient adds OVER I2S-only (cmplog), and what I2S adds OVER gradient-only (value_profile).

hypotheses.label: vpc assembles a LARGER, multi-field structure than I2S-only (cmplog) ever reaches; the gradient is necessary to grow size/token-count past where exact substitution stalls, and I2S is necessary because the gradient alone (value_profile) places ~0 structural tokens
hypotheses.prediction: On the value_profile-BLOCKING seed set the structural-token count is ~0 (vp_tags ~ 0): the gradient alone cannot place ANY exact multi-byte structural token, so I2S is necessary. AND the vpc-RESOLVING median seed is materially BIGGER than the cmplog-BLOCKING median seed (size_lift >= 1.3): the gradient grows the structure past where exact-substitution-only (cmplog) plateaus, so the gradient is necessary. A pure-I2S-substitution mechanism (no assembly depth) would predict size_lift ~ 1.0 (vpc and cmplog reach the SAME-sized structure, cmplog just hasn't placed the one tag yet); a pure-gradient mechanism would predict vp_tags > 0 (the gradient could place tokens on its own). Only the assembly-depth joint predicts BOTH vp_tags~0 AND size_lift>=1.3.

hypotheses.label: vpc and cmplog reach a SIMILARLY-sized structure (size_lift ~ 1, sometimes vpc smaller), but vpc places a specific gradient-climbed VALUE that the cmplog-blocking seeds approach yet never reach; I2S is still necessary to place the surrounding exact literal(s), and the gradient is necessary for the last-mile value precision the structural proxy is blind to

params.comment: Built tool value_distance_reached (descriptor_key seed_set:value_distance_reached:operand_literal:per_branch). For the branch's decisive CMP site, take the target operand value (operand_literal from the signature, e.g. 0x00020000 for harfbuzz/5609-5616, the 'loca'/'post' tag for 5440/5442, the interned name3 string for libxml2/6414, the chained language/script tag for 6214/6223). The tool computes the MIN over each arm's seeds of (min over sliding windows of Hamming(window, target_operand)/width); numeric mode for inequality gates. vpc_min_distance is the vpc-RESOLVING arm, cmp_min_distance the cmplog-BLOCKING arm; distance_closure_ratio = (cmp_min_distance - vpc_min_distance)/max(cmp_min_distance, 1).
params.operand_source: signature.operand_literal / operand_width_bytes; cmp-site offset from the per-role coverage overlay or the analysis byte-diff (the W-vs-L divergent offset)
params.feasibility: BUILT + validated (anchor sqlite3/14437; 4/4 local anchors winner_closer=true). Seed sets for this hypothesis's harfbuzz + libxml2 members exist (286-rep DB); all scorable.

hypotheses.prediction: Structures are the SAME size (size_lift ~ 1.0, the assembly-depth proxy is blind here) BUT the vpc-resolving seeds reach a value strictly CLOSER to the target operand than the cmplog-blocking seeds ever do: cmp_min_distance > 0 (cmplog seeds approach but never hit the exact value) while vpc_min_distance ~ 0 (vpc lands it), so distance_closure_ratio is large. A pure-I2S mechanism would predict cmp_min_distance ~ 0 too (cmplog HAS I2S, so if a single substitution sufficed cmplog would also reach the value and resolve -- contradicting the L label); the fact that cmplog approaches-but-misses is exactly what the gradient (value_profile) supplies. This rules out 'I2S alone is enough' AND, because size_lift~1, rules out the assembly-depth story.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 1). The cmplog-BLOCKING arm reaches a value that is STRICTLY CLOSER to the gate operand than naive/value_profile but still NON-zero, while the vpc-RESOLVING arm lands it (distance ~ 0): the value-profile CMP_MAP gradient supplies the last-mile closure on a fixed/string operand that cmplog's discrete I2S substitution stalls short of, and I2S is necessary because the gradient alone (value_profile) never reaches the operand window. Same-size structures (the assembly-depth size proxy is blind). This is the value-closure subtype the prior joint_value_precision rule under-captured because it cross-referenced size_lift (a different tool's metric) and used too strict a closure floor; this hypothesis scores entirely on value_distance_reached's OWN validated metrics.

params.comment: Reuses the BUILT value_distance_reached tool (descriptor_key seed_set:value_distance_reached:operand_literal:per_branch), scored ONLY on its own native metrics (no cross-tool size_lift dependency). Per-branch target operand from signature.operand_literal: harfbuzz/5609,5614,5616 -> 0x00020000 (width 4, derived_integer, numeric mode); lcms/2107 -> closest of {link,prtr,spac}; lcms/2184 -> closest of {link,abst}; lcms/2196 -> closest of {lams,link}; lcms/2209 -> closest of {lcms,link,prtr,spac,MCH1}; libxml2/7326 -> '<![CDATA[' (variable-length string, edit/prefix mode). All operands width>=4 so chance-alignment caveat (width<=2) does not apply.
params.operand_source: signature.operand_literal / operand_width_bytes; cmp-site offset from per-role overlay / analysis byte-diff (W-vs-L divergent offset, e.g. 0x18 version field for 5609/5614/5616, 0x0c device-class FOURCC for lcms).
params.feasibility: BUILT (value_distance_reached, validated). harfbuzz on-disk corpus on s4. lcms + libxml2 corpora are owned by server B (on-disk lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty); their resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds are in the 286-rep DB which is what the seed_set source consumes, so server B arbitrates them. Per cross-server rule these are NOT marked decidable:false for absence on s4.

hypotheses.prediction: The cmplog-blocking arm gets CLOSER to the operand than the gradient-less baselines yet stalls at cmp_min_distance > 0; the vpc-resolving arm closes it (vpc_min_distance ~ 0), so winner_closer == true and distance_gap > 0 with a substantial distance_closure_ratio. Discriminating: a PURE-I2S story predicts cmp_min_distance ~ 0 (cmplog has I2S; one substitution would land the fixed 4-byte constant / FOURCC and resolve -- contradicting the L label), so a non-zero cmp_min_distance with winner_closer rules out 'I2S alone is enough'. A PURE-GRADIENT story predicts value_profile reaches the window too; but vp blocks, so I2S-placed surrounding bytes are required. A pure ASSEMBLY-DEPTH story predicts winner_closer driven by a bigger structure, not a closer value -- here the structures are same-size and the gap is in the operand window, not token count. Only value-gradient-closure predicts winner_closer + non-zero cmp_min_distance + a same-size structure.
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true AND cmp_min_distance > 0 AND distance_closure_ratio >= 0.34

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 2). The value_profile CMP_MAP gradient is necessary as a CORPUS-RETENTION engine, not as a within-seed value climber: vpc ADMITS and HOARDS the near-miss intermediate inputs (operands progressively closer to the gate constant/structure) that bare cmplog's edge-only feedback discards because they do not flip a coverage bucket. The gradient's footprint is therefore a materially LARGER retained corpus in vpc than in cmplog -- the stepping-stone scaffold I2S then completes. I2S is necessary because the gradient-only arm (value_profile) still blocks (the L label): the scaffold is sterile without the exact-substitution stage that finally lands the operand. This is the subtype the seed-set-snapshot tools (value_distance_reached, joint_necessity) are STRUCTURALLY BLIND to: cmplog's discarded intermediates are absent from its FINAL seed set, so a final-snapshot distance/size comparison cannot see the retention difference; only a whole-corpus population count can.

params.comment: BUILT tool bench/tools/corpus_size_ratio.py (descriptor_key corpus_queue:corpus_size_ratio:vpc_vs_cmplog:per_trial). Whole-corpus, branch-INDEPENDENT comparison of two fuzzer ARMS over the target -- corpus_count_ratio = median(#saved seeds in the vpc queue) / median(#saved seeds in the cmplog queue) across trials. winner=value_profile_cmplog (gradient+I2S, resolves), loser=cmplog (I2S-only, blocks). RETENTION/INFLATION mechanism, NOT homogenization, so composition_entropy is not required.
params.operand_source: no per-branch operand needed -- operand-free and structural-token-free, which is why it could attempt harfbuzz/5980 (USE category-range/sequence) and libxml2/8161 (two-namespace structural-presence) that value_distance_reached and joint_necessity cannot.
params.feasibility: BUILT. Needs on-disk corpora for BOTH arms. harfbuzz arms on-disk on s4; lcms + libxml2 arms on-disk on server B. Per cross-server rule, NOT decidable:false for absence on the running server.

hypotheses.prediction: The vpc queue retains a materially LARGER saved corpus than the cmplog queue: corpus_count_ratio (vpc/cmplog) >= 1.3. DISCRIMINATING (G1): a PURE-I2S story (gradient adds nothing to corpus admission) predicts corpus_count_ratio ~ 1.0 and refutes; a homogenization story (different shape) predicts entropy collapse not count inflation. G2-CLEAN: reads only saved-corpus population, independent of the L,L,W,L resolve pattern.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 3). The gradient's necessity is visible as ITERATION/EXECUTION DEPTH at the decisive branch line: the vpc-RESOLVING corpus drives the gate line markedly MORE times per seed (and actually takes the decisive side) than the cmplog-BLOCKING corpus, which reaches only the gate's vicinity and fires the line a handful of times before stalling one comparison short of the loop/body it guards. Across these branches the cards uniformly report exactly this asymmetry in the per-role coverage overlay -- vpc branch-line hitcount 39/29/50/55089 against cmplog 3/13/5/1820 (harfbuzz/5609,5616; lcms/2196,2209) -- the gradient retains the near-miss inputs that let the corpus RE-ENTER and iterate the gated region, while cmplog's edge-only feedback keeps only the seeds that touch it shallowly. I2S is still necessary (value_profile alone, the L arm, never even reaches the line: hitcount ~0 in the cards), and the gradient is necessary because without it the cmplog corpus reaches the line but cannot drive it deep. This is a DIFFERENT measurement channel from value-distance (per-seed operand closeness), token-count (structure size), and corpus-population (queue scale): depth_reach RUNS each arm's corpus through the instrumented cov binary and reads the per-seed-normalized execution count of the branch LINE itself -- precisely the asymmetry the round-1/2 final-snapshot byte tools and the whole-corpus count tool are blind to, because a bigger/closer/more-populous corpus does not by itself imply the gate line is driven deeper per seed.

params.comment: BUILT tool bench/tools/depth_reach.py (descriptor_key seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch). Runs winner_fuzzer=value_profile_cmplog RESOLVING seeds and loser_fuzzer=cmplog BLOCKING seeds through the libafl-<target>-cov image, reads the branch line's per-seed-normalized execution count + blocked-side count from llvm-cov. depth_lift = winner_line_count / max(loser_line_count, 1) (per-seed normalized); side_lift = winner's take of the decisive side vs loser's; winner_deeper = depth_lift>1 (winner drives the line deeper / takes the side the loser does not). The winner/loser ARMS are the joint shape's vpc (resolves) vs cmplog (I2S-only, blocks) -- so the depth asymmetry isolates what the GRADIENT (the only delta between the two) adds on top of I2S: not 'does vpc resolve' (that would be G2-circular) but 'how DEEPLY does each arm's corpus drive the gated line'.
params.operand_source: no per-branch operand needed -- the branch LINE is the unit (cmscnvrt.c:646 / cmsio1.c:782 / cmslut.c:354,1620 / hb-ot-post-table.hh:141,223,255 / hb-ot-shaper-use-machine.hh:981 / hash.c:787 / tree.c:774). This is exactly why it can score harfbuzz/5980 (USE category-range case-10, no fixed operand) and libxml2/8161 (two-namespace presence, operand_literal=null) that value_distance_reached / joint_necessity could not.
params.feasibility: BUILT (depth_reach, validated on iteration-depth ctx 7/20). Docker per-branch (~30-60s) needs the target's libafl-<target>-cov image: harfbuzz + libxml2 cov images exist (docker/targets/Dockerfile.{harfbuzz,libxml2}.cov); lcms cov build is owned by server B. Needs resolving_seeds (vpc) + blocking_seeds (cmplog) in the 286-rep DB (present). Per cross-server rule, branches whose cov image/seeds live on the owning server are arbitrated there, NOT marked decidable:false for absence on s4.

hypotheses.prediction: The vpc-resolving corpus drives the decisive branch LINE markedly deeper per seed than the cmplog-blocking corpus: depth_lift >= 1.5 AND winner_deeper == true (the gradient retains the near-miss inputs that re-enter and iterate the gated region; the cards show vpc line-counts an order of magnitude above cmplog's). DISCRIMINATING (G1): a PURE-I2S story -- where the gradient adds nothing and the only vpc>cmplog delta is luck on the same I2S substitution -- predicts depth_lift ~ 1.0 (both corpora that touch the line touch it equally deep, the difference is just whether a single seed crossed); it CAN come out that way and would REFUTE this hypothesis. An ASSEMBLY-DEPTH alternative predicts the gap is in structure SIZE (size_lift, already scored EARLIER in the order) not in per-seed line iteration; a VALUE-PRECISION alternative predicts the gap is in operand closeness (value_distance, scored earlier) not line depth. Only the gradient-as-iteration-depth mechanism predicts depth_lift>>1 driven by the branch LINE being re-entered more per seed. G2-CLEAN: depth_reach reads what each arm's CORPUS reaches in the cov binary (per-seed line execution count), NOT the campaign resolve label -- a vpc corpus could resolve the branch while touching the line exactly as shallowly as cmplog (depth_lift~1), refuting; the measurement is therefore independent of the L,L,W,L resolve pattern that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 1, honest inconclusive; narrowed round 3). Joint gates whose discriminating content is a CATEGORY-SEQUENCE / range gradient or a multi-token STRUCTURAL-PRESENCE condition with NO single fixed operand value to measure distance to, AND for which NONE of the tried built measurements (token-count assembly proxy, whole-corpus retention ratio, branch-line iteration depth) fired on the owning server -- no remaining BUILT measurement can separate the I2S vs gradient contribution. Left honestly inconclusive (G3). This is the residual fallback AFTER joint_gradient_corpus_retention (corpus population) AND joint_gradient_iteration_depth (per-seed line depth) have both been tried: a branch lands here ONLY if both of those whole-corpus / depth tests also fail to hold on its owning server.

params.comment: harfbuzz/5980: USE-machine case-10 transition gated by a RANGE comparison _keys[0] <= use_category() <= _keys[1] over a category-code SEQUENCE; operand_kind=enum_or_code, operand_literal=null. There is no fixed target value for value_distance_reached, and no structural-token vocabulary for joint_necessity (per-glyph category prefix, not sfnt/FOURCC tags). libxml2/8161: 'second xmlNewNs on the same node' -- presence_or_nonnull on both h0 and h1, operand_literal=null; a multi-token structural-well-formedness condition the KEPT joint_assembly_depth (token_count) rule already scored and did not hold. Round 2 added the operand-free corpus_size_ratio test; round 3 adds the operand-free depth_reach test (per-seed branch-line iteration depth), BOTH of which CAN attempt 5980 and 8161. This hypothesis remains the honest fallback ONLY for the case where BOTH the corpus-retention ratio AND the iteration-depth lift also fail to hold. A relaxed-threshold re-test of any already-scored measurement is forbidden, and no other DIFFERENT built tool discriminates a structural-presence / range-sequence gate at the per-branch level.
params.feasibility: Fallback only. After joint_gradient_corpus_retention (corpus_size_ratio) and joint_gradient_iteration_depth (depth_reach) are both tried on the owning server, a branch meeting neither has no further built measurement that discriminates its I2S-vs-gradient split -> decidable:false. The arbiter leaves such a branch inconclusive rather than force-fit a mechanism.

hypotheses.prediction: No falsifiable per-branch operand-level prediction remains that an UNTRIED built tool could score: 5980 has no fixed operand for a distance metric and no structural tokens for a count metric; 8161's per-branch token_count already failed and cannot be relaxed. After the operand-free corpus-retention (round 2) and iteration-depth (round 3) whole-corpus/depth tests are both tried, a branch meeting neither is honestly decidable:false.

data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence on an 'I2S event' is used (I2S is untagged in mutation_op).

No byte-alignment / byte_freq_ratio is used (joint gates have different-SIZED W vs L seeds); value content is measured via value_distance_reached (operand-window Hamming/edit distance, alignment-free), structure via struct_size_and_token_count, the gradient's corpus footprint via whole-corpus corpus_size_ratio (a count/population comparison needing no byte alignment), and -- new in round 3 -- the gradient's iteration footprint via depth_reach (per-seed branch-LINE execution count from the cov binary, also alignment-free).

value_profile is treated as a FEEDBACK throughout (its effect read via what the corpus reaches: token count, value distance, retained-corpus size/population, and -- new round 3 -- the per-seed DEPTH the corpus drives the gated line to), never as a lineage gradient event.

depth_reach (joint_gradient_iteration_depth) RUNS the value_profile_cmplog resolving + cmplog blocking seeds through the libafl-<target>-cov image; cov images exist for harfbuzz + libxml2 (docker/targets/), and lcms's cov build is owned by server B. Per cross-server rule, branches are arbitrated by the server holding their cov image + seeds and are NOT decidable:false merely for absence on the running server.

joint_gradient_corpus_retention (corpus_size_ratio) reads ON-DISK corpora and needs BOTH the value_profile_cmplog and cmplog queues present; harfbuzz members scored on s4, lcms+libxml2 members on server B (cross-server).

Seed-set tools (joint_necessity, value_distance_reached, depth_reach) consume db/blockers.sqlite resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds (the 286 reps have them); cross-server, lcms+libxml2 members are arbitrated by server B.
notes:
ROUND-3 RE-DESIGN (FINAL, loop-until-dry). SUPERSET / FREEZE: all FIVE prior hypotheses carried forward VERBATIM (rules unchanged) so the 13 CONFIRMED branches re-validate -- KEEP: joint_assembly_depth [vp_tags<0.3 AND size_lift>=1.3], joint_value_precision [size_lift<1.3 AND cmp_min_distance>0 AND distance_closure_ratio>=0.5], joint_value_gradient_closure [winner_closer AND cmp_min_distance>0 AND distance_closure_ratio>=0.34], joint_gradient_corpus_retention [corpus_count_ratio>=1.3], and the decidable:false joint_sequence_or_structural_nondiscriminable [rule:false] (label text only narrowed to note that depth_reach is now also tried before it; rule unchanged). None re-tested, relabeled, or dropped.

PRIORS KEPT (5): joint_assembly_depth, joint_value_precision, joint_value_gradient_closure, joint_gradient_corpus_retention (the four decidable KEEP hypotheses, each confirmed >=1 branch in earlier rounds), plus the decidable:false joint_sequence_or_structural_nondiscriminable fallback.

NEW MECHANISM (1 decidable): joint_gradient_iteration_depth, using the BUILT-but-not-yet-tried-on-this-shape depth_reach tool (registry-listed). It targets all 10 still-unlabeled rule_not_met branches, whose h1 cards UNIFORMLY report a large branch-LINE hitcount divergence in the per-role coverage overlay -- vpc drives the decisive line FAR more times per seed than cmplog (concrete card numbers: harfbuzz/5609 vpc 39 vs cmplog 3; 5616 vpc 55089 vs cmplog 1820; lcms/2196 vpc 29 vs cmplog 13; lcms/2209 vpc 50 vs cmplog 5; lcms/2107 vpc avg-hit 80 vs cmplog 8) -- while value_profile (the L arm) never reaches the line at all. WHY they failed every prior rule AND why depth_reach is the right NEW channel: value_distance_reached / joint_necessity read the FINAL seed-set snapshot (operand closeness / token count) and corpus_size_ratio reads the whole-corpus POPULATION; NONE of those imply the gate LINE is re-entered more per seed. The gradient's necessity here is that it retains the near-miss inputs that let the corpus iterate INTO/THROUGH the gated region -- a per-seed execution-DEPTH property that only depth_reach (which RUNS the seeds and counts the line) can read. WHY NEW (G1) and not a relaxed prior: (a) a different TOOL (depth_reach, never tried on this shape) reading a different MEASUREMENT CHANNEL (per-seed branch-line execution count from the cov binary), not a threshold relaxation of any already-scored byte/population measurement; (b) DISCRIMINATING -- a pure-I2S 'gradient adds nothing, vpc just got lucky on the same substitution' alternative predicts depth_lift~1 and REFUTES; an assembly-depth alternative predicts the gap is in structure size (scored earlier), a value-precision alternative predicts it is in operand closeness (scored earlier); (c) G2-CLEAN -- depth_reach reads what each arm's CORPUS drives the LINE to, never the resolve label (a vpc corpus could resolve while touching the line as shallowly as cmplog -> depth_lift~1 -> refute). BONUS: because depth_reach is operand-free and structural-token-free, it gives harfbuzz/5980 (USE category-range case-10, no fixed operand) and libxml2/8161 (two-namespace presence, operand_literal=null) a SECOND operand-free falsifiable test (after corpus_size_ratio) before they fall back to inconclusive.

BRANCH-RESOLUTION CAVEAT: unlike corpus_size_ratio (a whole-corpus arm-pair scalar identical for every branch of a target), depth_reach is PER-BRANCH (it counts the specific decisive LINE, e.g. hb-ot-post-table.hh:223 vs :255 are different lines), so it CAN fine-separate the unlabeled branches from each other -- a real advantage over the round-2 catch-all. The decision_order still protects the 13 confirmed branches: they match their KEPT, more-specific per-branch rules EARLIER in the order and never reach the depth test.

HONEST decidable:false RESIDUAL (<=2 branches): joint_sequence_or_structural_nondiscriminable = harfbuzz/5980 + libxml2/8161, but ONLY if BOTH joint_gradient_corpus_retention (corpus population, tried 4th) AND joint_gradient_iteration_depth (line depth, tried 5th) fail to hold on their owning server. If either holds, they are labeled; if neither does, they remain honestly inconclusive (G3) -- no remaining built per-branch tool discriminates a range-sequence (no fixed operand) or structural-presence (operand_literal=null, token_count already failed, relaxation forbidden) gate's I2S-vs-gradient split.

DECISION ORDER: joint_assembly_depth (size+token conjunction) -> joint_value_precision (per-seed value closure>=0.5) -> joint_value_gradient_closure (per-seed native closure>=0.34) -> joint_gradient_corpus_retention (whole-corpus population inflation>=1.3) -> joint_gradient_iteration_depth (per-seed branch-LINE depth lift>=1.5, the round-3 per-branch discriminator for the gradient-retention/iteration subtype) -> joint_sequence_or_structural_nondiscriminable (decidable:false residual). A branch matching none falls through to fallback:inconclusive. CROSS-SERVER: harfbuzz + libxml2 (cov images + seeds) on s4/owning server; lcms on server B; depth_reach, corpus_size_ratio, and the seed-set tools each have every branch scorable by its OWNING server, so no branch is marked decidable:false merely for corpus/cov-image absence on s4.
